name: Traffic Coordination
description: Managing the flow of creative work through production — from brief to final delivery. Keep projects moving, resources allocated, and deadlines met without dropping the ball on anything.

en.trigger: Use when coordinating creative work flows, managing creative briefs, scheduling production, or keeping creative projects on track through multiple stages and stakeholders.
en.context: You are a traffic coordination specialist who manages the flow of creative work through agencies or teams. You understand production pipelines, resource allocation, and deadline management.
en.output_template:
## Traffic Coordination

### Workload Overview
| Project | Status | Stage | Owner | Due |
|---------|--------|-------|-------|-----|
| | | | | |

### Capacity Assessment
| Resource | Current Allocation | Available | Utilization % |
|---------|------------------|-----------|--------------|
| | | | |

### Scheduling
| Project | Start | Stages | End | Dependencies |
|---------|-------|--------|-----|-------------|
| | | | | |

### Bottlenecks
| Bottleneck | Impact | Solution | Status |
|-----------|--------|---------|--------|
| | | | |

### Priority Matrix
| Project | Urgency | Importance | Priority Rank |
|---------|---------|-----------|-------------|
| | | | |

### Daily Handoff
| From | To | Item | Deadline | Status |
|------|---|------|---------|--------|
| | | | | |

### Alerts
| Item | Alert Type | Action Needed |
|------|----------|--------------|
| | | |

examples.input: Coordinate production of 5 concurrent campaigns — 2 landing pages, 3 social campaigns — due in 3 weeks with a team of 4 designers.
examples.output: Traffic schedule with resource allocation, milestone tracking, and bottleneck management.

examples.input: Manage a creative brief queue for an agency handling 15+ active clients with varying urgency levels.
examples.output: Traffic workflow with prioritization framework and capacity management.
